titel: The EU is launching a support program to promote connection and community building with the aim of fostering peace in the South Caucasus region.
html:
European Commission Press Release Baku, July 1, 2026

Today, Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, together with Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos, met with Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev in Baku. During the meeting, they discussed ways to promote peace in the region through increased connectivity and support for communities near borders. They also addressed bilateral trade and energy cooperation agreements between the EU and Azerbaijan.
tldr: The EU is launching a new program to promote peace in the South Caucasus by improving cooperation between neighboring countries. The aim is to foster a sense of community and strengthen trade with Azerbaijan.
lattlast:
The European Union (EU) has launched a support program to foster peace in the South Caucasus region. By helping people living near borders collaborate more and build community, the aim is to reduce conflicts.

During a meeting in Baku, leaders from the EU and Azerbaijan discussed how this support will be implemented. They also talked about improving trade in goods and cooperation on energy matters between the two parties.

This program is part of the EU's efforts to create stability in the region. By connecting neighboring countries with better infrastructure and joint projects, the hope is to build a more secure future for everyone living there.
